desc: calcStats appends to the global resultList without clearing it, so repeated main or calcStats calls print stale and duplicate results.
calcStats and plotData dereference DATAFRAME without validating initialization, causing an AttributeError or TypeError when called before loadData.
plotData unconditionally calls plt.show(), which can block execution or fail in headless and automated environments.
calcStats records arbitrary or redundant values such as meanA_again, meanB_plus_42, and dummy row count as results, making its output semantically misleading.
Core functions communicate through mutable globals instead of parameters and return values, creating hidden ordering dependencies and making reuse and testing error-prone.
loadData generates data from global unseeded randomness with no injectable seed or generator, preventing reproducible results and deterministic tests.
tempStorage duplicates selected values from resultList but is never read or reset, adding persistent state with no functional purpose.
